title: Module 1
content:
Introduction - History of Lean – Toyota Production System (TPS)- Ford
Production System (FPS)- Principle of Lean Manufacturing- Seven Wastes,
their causes and the effects- Conventional Manufacturing versus Lean
Manufacturing

title: Module 2
content:
Tools of Lean manufacturing:
5-S, Workplace organization, Total Productive Maintenance, Process
mapping/ Value stream mapping, Work cell, Cause and Effect diagram,
Pareto chart, Spider chart, Poka yoke, Kanban, Automation, Single minute
exchange of die (SMED), Just in time (JIT), Visual workplace, OEE

title: Module 3
content:
Lean elements:
Introduction to Lean Concepts like In-Built Quality, Concept of Right Part
at the Right Time, Lead Time reduction, Optimum utilization of Capital,
Optimum utilization of People. Understanding the Zero-defect concept and
Metrics, Focus on Human Resources, Quality and Delivery Cost. Building
Zero defect capabilities, Cultural and Organizational aspects

title: Module 4
content:
Six Sigma Fundamentals:
Introduction to six sigma- basic tools of six sigma like problem solving
approach, standard deviation, normal distribution. DMAIV and DMADV.
Agile Manufacturing:
Agile manufacturing - Definition, business need, conceptual frame work,
characteristics, and generic features -Approaches to enhance ability in
manufacturing - Managing people in agile organization
